channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: TETFund inaugurates 25-man 2026 National Research Fair/Exhibition committee
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund) has reconstituted an expanded National Research Fair and Exhibition Committee for 2026, as part of efforts to accelerate the commercialisation of research findings, deepen collaboration between academia and industry, and drive job creation in Nigeria. The

item.title: We’ll bridge gap between academia, industry – Committee chairman
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Chairman of the reconstituted TETFund National Research Fair and Exhibition Committee, Umar Bindir, has pledged that the committee will work tirelessly to ensure that research outputs from Nigerian institutions are transformed into commercially viable products capable of creating jobs, reducing pove

item.title: Our target is to commercialise research, innovations
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund) has reaffirmed its commitment to bridging the gap between research and industry by reconstituting the committee for the third TETFund National Research Fair and Exhibition. The post Our target is to commercialise research, innovations appeared first on The

item.title: Israel sets October 27 election as Netanyahu faces crucial post-Gaza war test
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Israel will hold national elections on Oct. 27, the latest date permitted by law, parliament said on Sunday, setting up a vote widely seen as a referendum on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's leadership since the Gaza war began. Netanyahu, 76, said he intends to seek re-election and "win" another

item.title: TETFund set to spotlight AI, robotics at 2026 research fair
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: the Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund) has announced that participation in the 2026 National Research Fair and Exhibition will be open to all Nigerian innovators, regardless of educational background or location, as it seeks to uncover and commercialise home-grown technologies capable of drivin

item.title: Nigerian professionals launch app to bridge intelligence gaps in national security
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A group of Nigerian professionals under the auspices of the School of Politics, Policy and Governance (SPPG) has launched Synited Safe, a citizen-driven mobile and web application designed to facilitate anonymous or verified real-time reporting of crimes, suspicious activities and emergencies throug

item.title: Army intercepts truck conveying illicit drugs along Lagos-Calabar coastal road
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army’s 65 Battalion have intercepted a truck conveying a large consignment of illicit drugs along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road following actionable intelligence from a credible source. The intelligence-led operation resulted in the successful interception of the vehicle and

item.title: NRS reaffirms commitment to voluntary tax compliance one year after landmark reforms
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Nigeria Revenue Service (NRS) has reaffirmed its dedication to fostering voluntary tax compliance, exactly one year after the rollout of Nigeria’s comprehensive tax reform initiatives. In an earlier statement marking the anniversary, the NRS described taxpayer education as a critical pillar in s

item.title: The architecture of hope: Moving Nigeria beyond survivalist capitalism
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Every Monday morning, the Nigerian economic engine roars to life under circumstances that would paralyze less resilient societies. From the boardroom dynamics of Lagos to the vast industrial corridors fueling our domestic production, our private sector operates with a relentless tenacity. Yet, as we

item.title: Driven by Interest Income, 12 Banks Generated N7.2tn Gross Earnings in Q1
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede  Following the tightening of monetary policy by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) that significantly boosted banks’ interest earnings, Access Holdings Plc, Ecobank Transnational Incorporated and 10 other

item.title: World Bank, WTO, Others Call for Strengthening of Food, Energy Security
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Oluchi Chibuzor and Erobomhan Jessica The heads of the International Energy Agency (IEA), International Monetary Fund (IMF), World Bank Group and World Trade Organisation (WTO) have called on governments and

item.title: GIVE A CHRISTIAN A CHANCE
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: TUNJI ADEYEMI argues why APC should consider a Christian candidate in Kwara governorship race The publication of excerpts from Professor Dapo Thomas’ autobiography, Lagos Boy and Lagos Politics, has once

item.title: A diamond called Bart Nnaji: The long shadow of 70 year quiet builder
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: There is a particular kind of greatness that announces itself — titles read out at the top of programmes, chieftaincy drums, motorcades. The post A diamond called Bart Nnaji: The long shadow of 70 year quiet builder appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: Article URL: https://www.prohousingpgh.org/blog/new-report-modernizing-property-tax-assessments-in-allegheny-county Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48886881 Points: 4 # Comments: 0

item.title: Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of strikes between U.S. and Iran
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices rose and U.S. stock-index futures slipped on Sunday, after the U.S. and Iran continued their tit-for-tat attacks around the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend.

item.title: At 96.80% YtD, NGX Oil & Gas Emerges Best Performing Index
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kayode Tokede   For closing at 96.80 per cent Year-till-Date (YtD) growth as of July 10, the Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) Oil & Gas has outshined other indices to emerge as

item.title: EGG HARVESTING AND DESPERATE WOMEN
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Health authorities should do more to contain the problem For a long time, there have been reports that some fertility clinics in the country are harvesting eggs of young girls

item.title: Hardship: Meat sales drop as families switch to fish, egg, pomo
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The meat business in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) is facing one of its toughest periods in recent years as rising living costs continue to erode residents’ purchasing power. The FCT depends largely on livestock supplied from northern Nigeria and neighbouring countries. However, growing insecu

item.title: Kale: Geopolitical Shifts Offer Nigeria Historic Opportunity to Lead Africa’s Industrialisation
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: For many years, Nigeria has relied heavily on crude oil exports while importing many of the finished products it consumes, leaving much of the value creation, jobs and industrial opportunities

item.title: Bwari council chair pays N148.8m workers arrears
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Chairman of Bwari Area Council of the FCT Hon Joshua Ishaku Musa has cleared ten months workers’ arrears of the council with a total of N148, 851, 749:30. A statement by the Chief Press Secretary to the council chairman, Aliyu Abubakar, said the amount was cleared within three weeks of the chair

item.title: NDLEA nabs S’African woman using son as cover to traffic heroin
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has arrested a 38-year-old South African woman, Ms Will Jessica Ann, at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport (NAIA) Abuja, after she was found to have allegedly concealed 14 large blocks of heroin weighing 5.75 kilogrammes in her luggage, using he

item.title: Nigeria’s Crude Oil Output Hits Over Six-year High of 1.73m Bpd
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Meets 104% of OPEC quota allocation Emmanuel Addeh in Abuja  Nigeria’s crude oil and condensate production soared to an average of 1.735 barrels per day in the month of June 2026, representing positive

item.title: How police arrested self-kidnap suspect, accomplice
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has uncovered and foiled a staged kidnapping incident, leading to the arrest of the purported victim and one of his accomplices. The arrest, according to a brief made available to newsmen by the Police Public Relations Officer (PPRO), Abayomi Jimoh, took place in Akure.

item.title: Police demote officers caught in extortion viral video
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Delta State Police Command has demoted two of its officers, identified as Inspector Augustine Jeremiah and Corporal Miracle Ehirim, caught in a viral video extorting money from motorists in the state. The officers attached to C Division Asaba were captured in the viral video receiving money from

item.title: N’Central 2027: Security, alliances to decide Benue, Kogi, Kwara, Nasarawa, Niger, Plateau, FCT
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Political alignments and realignments are already defining the direction of the pendulum ahead of next year’s general elections, with the North Central geopolitical zone emerging as one of the most closely watched battlegrounds in the country. The post N’Central 2027: Security, alliances to decide

item.title: Brothel owner charged with recruiting women for prostitution
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Delta State Police Command has arraigned a brothel owner, Prince Chizoba Ojokwu, 63, before an Asaba Magistrate Court for allegedly allowing his premises to be used as a brothel and procuring women and girls for prostitution. The police in a statement by its spokesperson, Bright Edafe, said it a

item.title: Police nab 2 kidnap suspects, recover 26 goats in Yobe
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Yobe State Police Command has arrested two suspected members of a kidnapping syndicate operating in Gujba Local Government Area and recovered 26 goats believed to have been stolen. The command said the suspects were arrested following the escape of a kidnap victim, whose information led police o

item.title: Court jails 4 for raping minor in Kano
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A Kano State High Court has sentenced four young men to 10 years’ imprisonment each after convicting them of raping a minor. The trial judge, Justice Aisha Mahmud, handed down the sentence after finding the defendants guilty of the offence. In her judgement, Justice Mahmud held that the prosecution

item.title: After 56 days in captivity our children may not return to schools — Oriire parents
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: One relative, Fausat Akindele, said the incident had permanently changed many parents’ perception of the schools’ safety. The post After 56 days in captivity our children may not return to schools — Oriire parents appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Nigeria’s power crisis lingers despite $20bn World Bank loans
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: There are growing concerns over the implementation and effectiveness of World Bank-funded electricity projects in Nigeria, with stakeholders frowning at delays, poor execution, and rising debt and other hiccups. Besides, many Nigerians say the progress on the projects has been at snail speed and out

item.title: Africa’s informal sector hits $125bn – AfDB
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The African Development Bank (AfDB) has estimated the potential of Africa’s informal sector revenue to be around $125 billion, while calling on countries on the continent to make deliberate efforts to harness these potentials efficiently. Vice President for Economic Governance and Knowledge Manageme

item.title: Nigerians should adopt mini-grids to end $14bn annual expenses on self-generation
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE Managing Director of the Rural Electrification Agency (REA), Dr Abba Aliyu, has advocated the adoption of mini-grids among Nigerian households and businesses to halt the estimated $14 billion annually spent on self-generation. He pointed out that mini-grids are no longer just household rural ele

item.title: INEC Extends Deadline to Submit Presidential, N’Assembly List of Candidates
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: *APC: Extension shows opposition not ready for 2027 polls Adedayo Akinwale in Abuja  The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC), has approved the extension of the deadline for submission of lists

item.title: SK Hynix Shares Drop in Seoul After Much-Hyped US Trading Debut
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. shares declined in Seoul on Monday after the memory chipmaker’s highly anticipated US trading debut.

item.title: At least 27 dead as fire engulfs popular Bangkok pub near Chatuchak market
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: At least 27 people were killed and 63 injured, many critically, after a fire ripped through a popular pub in Bangkok.

item.title: US to Increase Mexico Sugar Imports to 1.15 Million Metric Tons
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The US Department of Agriculture estimates it will import as much as 1.15 million metric tons of Mexican sugar in the 2026-2027 period, according to a statement posted on X by Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum.

item.title: Obidient Movement Accuses Keyamo of Publishing ‘Doctored’ Obi’s CCTV Footage
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: *Says APC persecuting presidential hopeful Sunday Ehigiator  The Obidient Movement has accused the Minister of Aviation and Aerospace Development, Festus Keyamo, of publishing a “doctored” CCTV footage in a bid

item.title: Ask HN: Add flag for AI-generated articles
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Should HN add the ability to flag articles as AI-generated? This doesn't have to act as a regular flag, i.e., it won't de-rank the article; it could just show up as an indicator, allowing others (like myself) who don't like reading AI-generated text, to skip it.Open questions:1. Why is the regular v

item.title: Obi Urges African Leaders to Create Conditions to Bring Diaspora Talent Home
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Sunday Aborisade in Abuja Presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Mr. Peter Obi, has urged African leaders to create an enabling environment that would encourage professionals in the

item.title: Japan’s pet care industry booms as ‘fur babies’ outnumber infants
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Japanese baby product manufacturers are turning their attention to the pet market amid collapsing birthrate.

item.title: Oil cartels, tanker operators, enforcement agencies blamed for Apapa, Kirikiri gridlock
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Oil cartels, independent petroleum marketers, tanker owners, drivers, transport unions and enforcement agencies have been blamed for Apapa traffic The post Oil cartels, tanker operators, enforcement agencies blamed for Apapa, Kirikiri gridlock appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Treasury Two-Year Yield Rises to Highest Since 2025 on Oil Gain
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Treasury two-year yield climbed to its highest level in more than 16 months as renewed tensions in Iran pushed up oil prices, fanning speculation the Federal Reserve will tighten policy to keep inflation in check.

item.title: ‘Trump accounts’ are great — if you’re already rich
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: They’re a poor deal for most other people.

item.title: US Says It Conducted Fresh Round of Strikes Against Iran
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The US releases videos from unidentified locations, saying its forces completed a third round of strikes against Iran. It prompted retaliatory strikes from Tehran. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Manufacturing leads Q1 VAT surge on improved tax administration —NBS
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: NIGERIA’S Value Added Tax (VAT) collections recorded robust growth in the first quarter of 2026, reaching ₦2.42 trillion, with the manufacturing sector maintaining its position as the largest contributor, accounting for 29.75 percent of total receipts, according to data released by the National Bure

item.title: Can Nigeria’s drone industry deliver Africa’s defence sovereignty
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Military manufacturing may be growing, but defence sovereignty depends on far more than production.

item.title: Black weekend: Family of nine, 18 others killed in Benue, Plateau
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “After killing two people in Ikpachi on Friday, this morning, they attacked Otukpo Nobi killing 16 persons including women and children" The post Black weekend: Family of nine, 18 others killed in Benue, Plateau appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Govt should clear drainage, enforce laws, save us from flood!
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Over the past few weeks, some parts of Lagos, such as Ilaje, Mushin, Victoria Island, Lekki, Lagos Mainland among others have experienced flood. Viral videos have shown houses been overthrown by water, destruction of vehicles and other properties. This has also led to a lot of people vacating their

item.title: Oriire Schoolchildren: How arrest of kidnappers’ family members led victims’ rescue
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "The family members were subsequently taken to Abuja, where the wives were interrogated in accordance with investigative procedures" The post Oriire Schoolchildren: How arrest of kidnappers’ family members led victims’ rescue appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: US launches more strikes on Iran as Strait of Hormuz standoff deepens
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran targeting Tehran's ability to threaten shipping in the strait.

item.title: Fontainebleau forest near Paris hit by 'virulent' wildfire
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire south of Paris scorched about 800 hectares of the Fontainebleau forest, prompting France to deploy two firefighting aircraft as crews battled to contain the blaze, officials said on Monday. The fire forced partial closures of the A6 motorway during a busy summer holiday weeken

item.title: Banks slash lending, cut N5.4trn across key sectors
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Banks slashed lending to oil and gas, information and communication technology (ICT) and six other key sectors of the economy by N5.45 trillion The post Banks slash lending, cut N5.4trn across key sectors appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Needless rush to build new airports
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: With over N500 billion reportedly spent by state governments in building new airports, most of them considered unprofitable, the recent warning by the Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN) against embarking on new airports without having a feasibility assessment is timely and should be heeded

item.title: Season of Selling to End Philippine Peso Rally, Strategists Say
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Philippine peso’s relief rally following the initial US-Iran ceasefire is likely to be short-lived as seasonal selling drives the currency to new record lows, strategists say.

item.title: Iran war live: US bombs Iranian cities again as Hormuz standoff intensifies
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iranian state media report explosions in port cities of Bandar Abbas, Sirik and Jask, as well as Qeshm Island.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Esther Onyegbula A new premium ready-to-drink non-alcoholic beverage, Ikoyi Chapmans, is set to enter the Nigerian market ahead of a planned expansion across Africa following the formal unveiling of a strategic partnership between its promoters and CAADES Group. The partnership was formalised on

item.title: Kwara trains 700 students, 130 teachers in AI, robotics
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Etop Ekanem, ILORIN No fewer than 700 students and 130 teachers in Kwara State have received hands-on training in artificial intelligence (AI), robotics and other digital skills at the 2026 edition of STEM Africa Fest, held at the Ilorin Innovation Hub. The two-day programme, held on July 2 and 4

That is the uncomfortable truth of the “Presidential Foreign Intervention Pr

item.description: By Ademola Lawrence When the news first broke that students and teachers had been abducted, the nation reacted as expected. Social media erupted with outrage. item.description: The veteran Republican says he will not return to work "quite yet", in his first statement following weeks of speculation about his health.

item.title: Gold Drops as US-Iran Strikes Revive Inflation, Rate-Hike Risks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold declined after the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes over the weekend, sending energy prices higher and once more raising the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

item.title: US launches new wave of strikes against Iran, aimed at ‘degrading’ military
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Sunday's attacks follow three previous days of tit-for-tat strikes between Iran and US as a June ceasefire deal frays.

item.title: Ratajski beats Wattimena to lift European Darts Open title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Krzysztof Ratajski defeated Jermaine Wattimena 8-6 in a nail-biting final to win the European Darts Open in Leverkusen.

item.title: World Cup 2026: The song everybody is singing but leaves the artiste in chains
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The song itself is an irony. While it has enabled millions to free themselves from desire and enter a state of euphoric Buddhist Nirvana across the globe, it has left the singer in tatters. Gala may be tired, as she always says, but she has not given up and is still fighting to recover the rights to

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The report said the improved performance was primarily driven by stable production operations across most producing assets and the absence of any major pipeline outages during the period under review.

item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The US military on Sunday said it had launched a new wave of attacks against Iran, after renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz saw several of Washington's Gulf allies targeted by incoming fire. The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at e

item.title: NIMC Registers 137m Nigerians As Tinubu Orders Full NIN Enrolment
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) has enrolled more than 137 million Nigerians into the National Identification Number (NIN) database as President Bola Tinubu directed the commission to ensure every Nigerian is registered before the end of 2026. item.title: Massive Bangkok pub fire kills at least 27 people, Thai officials say
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 27 people were killed and 63 injured after a fire tore through a Bangkok bar late on Sunday, Thai officials said. Bangkok Governor Chadchart Sittipunt said the blaze spread rapidly, with smoke believed to be the main cause of death. Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause.

item.title: Bellingham and Tuchel's ‘battle of wills’ is healthy for England
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Passionate, driven, uncompromising. Those are terms that can be equally applied to both Thomas Tuchel and Jude Bellingham.

Speaking on Sunday as the guest

item.title: Alavin Withdraws Claims Against Wizkid’s Associate Godson
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Emmanuel Jakpa, popularly known as Alavin, has withdrawn allegations contained in a viral video against businessman and social media influencer Uyi Godson Agho, popularly known as Godson, urging members of the public to disregard the recording. The retraction comes days after Godson, an associate of

item.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

item.title: Uruguay announce Diego Forlan as new coach
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Uruguay has appointed legendary former striker Diego Forlan as interim head coach of the national team following Marcelo Bielsa's resignation after La Celeste's disappointing group-stage exit at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post Uruguay announce Diego Forlan as new coach appeared first on Vanguard N

item.title: Oil Climbs, Treasuries Drop After US Strikes Iran: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil rose and Treasuries fell after the US launched another round of strikes against Iran, reviving concern that higher energy prices will keep inflation elevated and interest rates higher for longer.

item.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: A spokesman for the 71-year-old South Carolina senator says initial findings suggest Graham's cause of death was related to his heart.

item.title: Oil Jumps as Conflict Over Hormuz Escalates With Fresh Strikes
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil jumped as the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, with the sides offering conflicting statements on whether the Strait of Hormuz remains open.

item.title: Tinubu Has Given NIMC Until End Of 2026 To Enrol Every Nigerian, Says DG
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: She stressed that the National Identification Number (NIN) remains a unique identifier, ensuring that every individual is registered only once. The post Tinubu Has Given NIMC Until End Of 2026 To Enrol Every Nigerian, Says DG appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: MeCure Industries seeks N50bn capital raise as profit jumps 177%
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: MeCure Industries Plc has proposed increasing its capital-raising programme to N50 billion from N30 billion as the pharmaceutical manufacturer accelerates its expansion across Nigeria and West Africa. The proposal followed the company’s impressive 2025 financial performance, with revenue risin

item.title: FG arrests two suspects in Osun illegal mining crackdown
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government has intensified its nationwide crackdown against illegal mining with the arrest of two suspected illegal miners during a coordinated enforcement operation at an illegal mining site in Osun State. The Special Assistant on Media to the Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Lar

item.title: Detained by settlers, US Democrat Ro Khanna now faces pro-Israel attacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Progressive congressman accuses Israeli military of 'lying' after settlers held up his van in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: World No. 1 defends Wimbledon title
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Jannik Sinner is Wimbledon champion once again! The world No. 1 defeated Alexander Zverev 6-7, 7-6, 6-3, 6-4 in a thrilling four-set final to defend his title. The turning point came when Zverev slipped and appeared to injure his knee in the third set, allowing Sinner to seize control. The Italian k

item.title: Arsenal want Argentina's Alvarez - Monday's gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Arsenal want to sign Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez, Aston Villa hijack Newcastle's move for Johan Manzambi, plus more.
